talk_metadata.talk_title: Systemic reprogramming in cancer cachexia using Drosophila and mouse models
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we believe that the paraneoplastic effect of the tumor goes beyond muscle and fat wasting"
Speaker said: "we saw that most of the biomass of the growing tumor actually derived from the degrading organs and not food"
Speaker said: "we identified one that behaved exactly like this, which is called subermesa... reduces malignant tumor growth but does not affect benign growth or normal development"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Redundancy with SLC7A9; targeting SLC7A11 alone may be insufficient due to compensatory SLC7A9 activity

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker showed that SLC7A9 can compensate for SLC7A11 loss, suggesting limited monotherapy efficacy
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Focusing on SLC7A11 alone may dilute therapeutic signal due to functional redundancy with SLC7A9

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that tumor autophagy provides nutrients to support growth, then concluded that targeting autophagy alone will block tumor growth – ignoring that nutrient transporters (SLC7A9) can bypass autophagy-derived supply

key_data_gaps: No in vivo efficacy data for combined SLC7A9 inhibition with autophagy blockade
Lack of patient-derived validation of SLC7A9 dependency

crispro_opportunity.description: Targeting SLC7A9 alongside SLC7A11 could overcome redundancy and prevent tumor utilization of host-derived cysteine
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker identified subermesa (fly ortholog of SLC7A9) as essential for malignant tumor growth but not for benign growth
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model transporter network dependencies and predict combinatorial inhibition of SLC7A9/SLC7A11 to block cysteine uptake
external_validation_needed: PubMed: functional studies of SLC7A9 in pancreatic cancer

crispro_opportunity.description: Expression levels of SLC7A9 may stratify patients who would benefit from HIF2 or ferroptosis-targeted therapies
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Higher SLC7A9 expression correlated with tumor growth and ferroptosis resistance in pancreatic cancer models
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate transcriptomic data to develop a predictive biomarker panel including SLC7A9
external_validation_needed: TCGA analysis of SLC7A9 expression in RCC and pancreatic cancer
Prospective cohort measuring SLC7A9 protein levels

cited_competitors.name: SLC7A11 inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: Discussed as existing targets that may be insufficient alone

unresolved_questions: Can combined inhibition of autophagy and SLC7A9 produce synergistic tumor suppression?
What upstream signals regulate subermesa/SLC7A9 expression in cachectic tumors?

talk_metadata.talk_title: VHL loss–driven HIF‑2α–PTHrP axis mediates cachexia in clear‑cell renal carcinoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the phenotype was independent from the tumor size"
Speaker said: "treatment with HIF‑2α inhibitor reduced PTHrP levels and improved body weight"
Speaker said: "PTHLH knockout mice maintained body weight and survived"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Cachexia improvement observed but overall survival benefit not demonstrated in current trials
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Patients maintained weight gain independent of tumor response

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Inhibition of HIF‑2α to suppress PTHrP‑mediated cachexia
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Patients were not stratified by baseline PTHrP levels, potentially diluting weight‑gain signal
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Weight change analysis not powered for subgroup (high PTHrP) outcomes

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker demonstrated that PTHrP reduction is the primary cachexia mechanism, suggesting that targeting downstream PTHrP may be more direct
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Focusing on HIF‑2α may miss patients whose cachexia is driven by alternative pathways

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that PTHrP drives both hypercalcemia and weight loss, then concluded that HIF‑2α inhibition improves weight independent of tumor burden – ignoring that tumor burden itself can also influence cachexia

key_data_gaps: Long‑term survival impact of cachexia reversal
Effect of PTHrP neutralization independent of HIF‑2α inhibition

crispro_opportunity.description: Baseline plasma PTHrP could identify RCC patients who would benefit most from HIF‑2α inhibition for cachexia mitigation
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: High pretreatment PTHrP levels correlated with lower muscle mass and higher calcium in patients
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can develop a predictive model integrating PTHrP, calcium, and gene expression to select patients for HIF‑2α therapy
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: prospective trial stratifying by PTHrP
PubMed: validation of PTHrP as cachexia biomarker in RCC

crispro_opportunity.description: Direct PTHrP neutralizing antibodies or ligand traps could bypass HIF‑2α and directly treat cachexia
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: PTHLH knockout eliminated cachexia despite tumor presence
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model ligand‑receptor interaction networks to prioritize anti‑PTHrP biologics
external_validation_needed: Preclinical studies of anti‑PTHrP antibodies in RCC models

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine HIF‑2α inhibitors with agents that block downstream PTHrP signaling (e.g., PTH1R antagonists) to enhance cachexia reversal
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: HIF‑2α inhibition reduced PTHrP but did not improve overall survival
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate combination effects on cachexia endpoints
external_validation_needed: Preclinical combination studies of belzutifan + PTH1R antagonist
Phase I safety of combined regimen

cited_competitors.name: Belzutifan (Bayer)
cited_competitors.context: FDA‑approved HIF‑2α inhibitor evaluated for cachexia effects

cited_competitors.name: NKT2152 (Niken Therapeutics)
cited_competitors.context: Second HIF‑2α inhibitor showing PTHrP reduction

cited_competitors.name: Immune checkpoint inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: Compared as control arm; did not reduce PTHrP

cited_competitors.name: VEGF‑TK inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: Control arm; decreased BMI despite treatment

unresolved_questions: Does long‑term PTHrP suppression translate into overall survival benefit?
What is the precise mechanism by which PTHrP induces muscle wasting beyond lipolysis?
Can PTHrP levels predict response to HIF‑2α inhibitors in earlier disease stages?
